text:
🇦🇪 UAE — TAX STRUCTURE
1. Why this matters
Defines tax exposure on rental income, property sale, ownership holding costs, and transfer implications for non-resident individual investors.
2. Scope Note
(Official Context)
This note applies to non-resident individual ownership of real estate and does not cover corporate structuring or entity-based ownership.
3. Taxes Not Imposed
(Official Fact)
The United Arab Emirates does not impose federal personal income tax on rental income earned by individuals.
(Official Fact)
There is no capital gains tax imposed on disposal of real estate by individuals at the federal level.
(Official Fact)
There is no withholding tax on rental income or property-related payments to non-residents.
(Official Fact)
There is no estate or inheritance tax imposed on property ownership in the UAE.
4. Charges That Still Apply
(Official Fact)
Property transactions are subject to emirate-level transfer fees.
Dubai:
Transfer fee of 4% of property value, payable to Dubai Land Department (DLD)
(Advisory Caution)
Transfer-related administrative charges may vary depending on emirate and transaction structure.
5. Property-Related Ongoing Costs
(Official Fact)
There is no annual property tax at the federal level.
(Official Fact)
Emirate-level charges may apply, including:
Municipality fee (e.g., Dubai municipality fee typically applied on rental value)
Service charges payable to developer or building management
(Advisory Caution)
These charges are administrative or service-related costs, not taxes on ownership, and vary by property, developer, and emirate.
6. Succession / Inheritance Note
(Official Fact)
The UAE does not impose inheritance tax on property.
(Official Fact)
Property succession is governed by:
UAE personal status laws, or
Applicable foreign law (subject to registration and legal arrangements for non-Muslim expatriates)
(Advisory Caution)
Succession outcomes may depend on registration of wills and applicable jurisdictional rules.
7. Market Practice 
(Market Practice)
Municipality fees and service charges are commonly treated by investors as recurring holding costs associated with property ownership.
8. What Needs Legal Confirmation 
Applicable emirate-level fees for a specific transaction
Municipality fee calculation method for specific property
Service charge obligations under developer or community
Succession treatment based on registered will or jurisdiction
9. Sources
https://tax.gov.ae
https://dubailand.gov.ae
10. Verification Date
13/03/2026
11. Escalation Note
Not Required –
Tax framework is clearly defined at federal level, and emirate-level charges are administrative and known. Variations depend on property and jurisdiction, not unresolved legal ambiguity.
